subject: PATIENT
heading: History of Present Illness :: Interim History:__page_no__1
text: history of present illness interim history: transition of care inpatient name of the hospital [facility_name] 09/09/2024-09/20/2024 transition from rehabilitation facility [facility_name] 09/20/2024- 10/03/2024 transition to home (own) admission date 09/09/2024 30 day readmission no number of in patient admissions in last 12 months. 1 discharge date 10/03/2024 discharge summary report received yes per aultman dc summary on 09/20/2024: hospital course this is a 72-year-old male who was admitted on 9/9/2024 after experiencing a crush injury to his left lower extremity. he presented as a trauma and was admitted under trauma service. he had a full trauma workup with scans listed below. patient was admitted to sicu and was taken to the operating room with vascular surgery due to vascular injury and orthopedic surgery. he had an external fixator placed. given the severity of his injuries he then required a left above-the-knee amputation on 9/13/2024. wound vac was placed postoperatively and required change in the or on 9/16/2024. after his latest wound vac change in the or patient had met all discharge criteria from a trauma standpoint and orthopedic standpoint. patient remained hospitalized awaiting insurance approval for rehabilitation on 9/20/2024 rehabilitation was approved and patient was discharged to woodlawn rehab. he was to follow-up with orthopedic surgery in 1 week post hospitalization. date discharge summary was received: 09/20/2024 final discharge diagnoses received yes discharge diagnosis unspecified injury of popliteal artery, left leg unspecified fracture of left femur unspecified injury of popliteal vein, left leg acidosis, unspecified rhabdomyolysis unspecified fracture of lower end of unspecified femur, unspecified dislocation of left hip, initial encounter unspecified fracture of lower end of left femur effusion, left knee unspecified acquired deformity of left thigh acute sinusitis, unspecified contusion of left thigh, initial encounter contusion of left foot, initial encounter other disorder of circulatory system

entity: crush injury to his left lower extremity
subject: PATIENT
heading: Hospital Course__page_no__10
text: hospital course this is a 72-year-old male who was admitted on [encounter_date] after experiencing a crush injury to his left lower extremity. he presented as a trauma and was admitted under trauma service. he had a full trauma workup with scans listed below. patient was admitted to sicu and was taken to the operating room with vascular surgery due to vascular injury and orthopedic surgery. he had an external fixator placed. given the severity of his injuries he then required a left above-the-knee amputation on [encounter_date]. wound vac was placed postoperatively and required change in the or on [encounter_date]. after his latest wound vac change in the or patient had met all discharge criteria from a trauma standpoint and orthopedic standpoint. patient remained hospitalized awaiting insurance approval for rehabilitation.

text: ct angiography abd/pelvis/bilat lower ex result date: [encounter_date] verified by: [provider_name] clinical statement: impression: arterial: 1. severe narrowing of the left distal superficial femoral artery as itenters the adductor canal with luminal irregularity. the popliteal artery isessentially non-opacified. there is extravasation of contrast material inthe popliteal fossa as well as moderate hematoma formation within the deepmusculature posterior to the knee. findings compatible with acute arterialinjury. the remaining arteries distal to the superficial femoral artery asit enters the adductor canal are not opacified on the left.2. asymmetric enlargement of the left lower extremity at the level of theleft knee, likely due to acute arterial injury, hematoma and soft tissueedema. suggest to correlate with compartment syndrome.non-arterial: 1. displaced distal left femoral diaphysis fracture as above. entity: inability to palpate a pulse to the left lower extremity
subject: PATIENT
heading: Assessment/Plan__page_no__15
text: assessment/plan this patient is a 72-year-old male who presented to [facility_name] on [encounter_date] after his left lower extremity was pinned by a motor vehicle while he and his friends were attempting to work on the vehicle. he was upgraded to a trauma alert given the inability to palpate a pulse to the left lower extremity. x-rays were obtained and showed a left femur fracture. he was noted to be hypotensive in the trauma bay; however he was responsive to iv fluid administration.

subject: PATIENT
heading: Chief Complaint__page_no__15
text: chief complaint crush injury

subject: PATIENT
heading: History of Present Illness__page_no__15
text: history of present illness ttiis is d split snared visil between myself and dr. koberts this patient is a 72-year-old male with a past medical history significant for hypercholesterolemia, hypothyroidism, overactive bladder, bph, and obesity who presented to [facility_name] on [encounter_date] as a trauma standby after his left lower extremity was pinned by a car while he and his friend were attempting to work on the vehicle. he was upgraded to a trauma alert secondary to the inability to palpate a pulse to the left lower extremity. upon arrival to the trauma bay, the patient was awake, alert, oriented and able to answer questions appropriately. he denied loss of consciousness and stated that his friend was able to quickly able to remove the car off of his leg. he noted decreased sensation to his left lower extremity. he was able to wiggle his toes bilaterally. he was hypotensive; however, was responding to iv fluid administration. x-rays of the left lower extremity were obtained in the emergency department showing displaced fracture of the left femur. chest and pelvic x-rays pelvic x-rays were also obtained and were negative for any acute abnormality per the report.

text: history of present illness ttiis is d split snared visil between myself and dr. koberts this patient is a 72-year-old male with a past medical history significant for hypercholesterolemia, hypothyroidism, overactive bladder, bph, and obesity who presented to [facility_name] on [encounter_date] as a trauma standby after his left lower extremity was pinned by a car while he and his friend were attempting to work on the vehicle. he was upgraded to a trauma alert secondary to the inability to palpate a pulse to the left lower extremity. upon arrival to the trauma bay, the patient was awake, alert, oriented and able to answer questions appropriately. he denied loss of consciousness and stated that his friend was able to quickly able to remove the car off of his leg. he noted decreased sensation to his left lower extremity. he was able to wiggle his toes bilaterally. he was hypotensive; however, was responding to iv fluid administration. x-rays of the left lower extremity were obtained in the emergency department showing displaced fracture of the left femur. chest and pelvic x-rays pelvic x-rays were also obtained and were negative for any acute abnormality per the report.
